meta.name: Soul Food Salmon Croquettes
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Classic salmon croquettes/patties made with canned salmon, aromatics, and a crisp pan-fry. Includes optional cracker crumbs and hot sauce.

meta.summary: Crispy-edged salmon croquettes with onion, celery, herbs, and seasoning—quick soul food favorites for breakfast or dinner.

notes: Croquettes are fully cooked when hot throughout and browned on both sides.
If using fresh cooked salmon, ensure it was cooked safely before mixing.

ingredients.label: Canned salmon, drained (bones/skin removed if desired)
All-purpose flour (optional, if mixture is too wet)

steps.title: Prep salmon
steps.body: Drain canned salmon. Flake into a mixing bowl with a fork. Remove large bones/skin if you prefer (small bones are edible and mash easily).

steps.title: Mix aromatics and binders
steps.body: Add onion, celery, bell pepper (optional), parsley (optional), eggs, mayonnaise, mustard (optional), and hot sauce (optional). Mix until combined.

steps.title: Season and set texture
steps.body: Add breadcrumbs (or crushed crackers), salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and cayenne (optional). Mix. If mixture seems too wet to shape, add 1–2 tbsp flour until it holds together.

steps.title: Rest mixture
steps.body: Let mixture rest 10 minutes so crumbs hydrate and the mix firms up for shaping.

steps.title: Shape croquettes
steps.body: Form into 8–10 patties (about 1/2-inch thick). Press edges so they don’t crack.

steps.title: Pan-fry until crisp
steps.body: Heat oil in a skillet over medium to medium-high. Fry croquettes 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp. Work in batches; don’t overcrowd.

steps.title: Drain and hold
steps.body: Transfer to a rack (preferred) or paper towels. Keep warm while frying remaining batches.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Serve hot with grits, eggs, rice, or a side salad. Optional: add hot sauce or a squeeze of lemon.
